revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Jōyama Yokoanabo Cluster]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Jōyama Yokoanabo Cluster]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Jōyama Yokoanabo Cluster]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Jōyama Yokoanabo Cluster]] {{Infobox ancient site | name =Jōyama Yokoanabo Cluster | native_name = 城山横穴群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption =Jōyama Yokoanabo Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|40|43.88|N|130|46|47.87|E||display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|qid=Q1205781|lt=Fukuchi, Fukuoka|1=Fukuchi, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=cave tomb|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Yokoanabo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built =6th to 7th century AD | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = 2003-2013 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|40|43.88|N|130|46|47.87|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Jōyama Yokoanabo Cluster'''|城山横穴群|Jōyama yokoana-gun}} is an {{ill|Archaeological site|en|Archaeological site|qid=Q839954|lt=Archaeological site|1=Archaeological site}} containing a [[Kofun period]] {{nihongo|{{ill|corridor-type kofun|lt=Yokoanabo|kk=-type ''kofun''|ja|横穴墓|1=Yokoanabo|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-type kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoanabo|qq=}}|横穴墓|Yokōbo}} located in the Town of {{ill|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|en|Fukuchi, Fukuoka|qid=Q1205781|lt=Fukuchi, Fukuoka|1=Fukuchi, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. was granted protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 2014.<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/284766|title=城山横穴群 |work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=5 December 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Jōyama Yokoanabo Cluster is located in the upper reaches of the Onga River basin, and is built on an independent hill at the confluence of the Hikoyama River and the Chumotoji River with an elevation of 29 to 31 meters. The site stretches approximately 360 meters from north-to-south and approximately 100 meters from east-to-west. In an [[archaeological excavation]] conducted by the Fukuchi Town Board of Education from 2003 to 2013, 222 corridor-type ''kofun'', 12 mounds associated with corridor-type ''kofun'' and one corridor-type stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] tumulus were confirmed. Most of the tombs were found to be in good preservation, and a few contained human remains and shards of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} pottery. There are only four examples of corridor-type ''kofun'' clusters with over 200 graves in Kyushu, and this group has the highest density. Corridor-type ''kofun'' first appeared in this area in the first half of the 6th century. The peak of construction was around the end of the 6th century to the beginning of the 7th century, and funerals continued until at least the latter half of the 7th century. Furthermore, from the early to mid-sixth century, burials were mainly built in the northern part of the hill, but from the latter half of the sixth century, when the number of tombs started to increase, corridor-type ''kofun'' expanded to the southern part of the hill.
Currently, the ruins have been backfilled for protection after being surveyed. The site is 700 meters southeast of {{ill|Kanada Station|en|Kanada Station|qid=Q11647733|lt=Kanada Station|1=Kanada Station}} on the {{ill|Heisei Chikuhō Railway|en|Heisei Chikuhō Railway|qid=Q3943894|lt=Heisei Chikuhō Railway|1=Heisei Chikuhō Railway}}, or a seven-minute walk.
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|qid=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}}
